seo_title: Calibrated Interactive RL: Mitigating Distribution Shift in…
meta_description: Researchers propose a unified framework to address context distribution shift in multi-turn dialogue systems, reducing the sim-to-real gap and mitigating…
body_html:
Direct answer: Researchers propose a unified framework to address context distribution shift in multi-turn dialogue systems, reducing the sim-to-real gap and mitigating compounding distribution shifts.

Key Challenges

  • Policy-induced shift: training on static histories rather than self-generated trajectories
  • Simulator-induced shift: discrepancies between simulated and real human behaviors

Calibrated Interactive RL Framework

Our approach couples interactive RL with simulator alignment to reduce the sim-to-real gap and mitigate compounding distribution shifts.

Experimental Results

MethodPerformance Metric
Interactive RL State-of-the-art downstream performance
Calibrated Interactive RL Improved dialogue quality and reduced distribution shift

Conclusion

Our approach establishes a unified framework to address context distribution shift in multi-turn dialogue systems, yielding state-of-the-art downstream performance.

source.title: From Static Context to Calibrated Interactive RL: Mitigating Distribution Shift in Multi-turn Dialogue with Aligned Simulator
source.summary: Researchers have identified a significant challenge in developing highly interactive dialogue agents: context distribution shift, which occurs when training data and real conversations diverge. This mismatch can severely degrade dialogue quality as it compounds over turns. To address this issue, the authors propose Calibrated Interactive RL, a unified framework that aligns simulators with human interaction patterns to reduce the gap between simulated and real behaviors. Experiments show that this approach outperforms existing methods in mitigating distribution shift and achieving state-of-the-art performance.
